metadata.title: Why splitting a chunk from its document makes it retrieve for the wrong question
metadata.purpose: Generate a short document-level summary and prepend it to each chunk before embedding, so the vector carries both local content and its document context and resolves ambiguous token matches.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: chunking strategies, embedding-model comparisons, full-pipeline benchmarking
metadata.visual_object: Side-by-side bare chunk vs context-prepended chunk with a hit/miss matrix across 10 queries
metadata.hook: A chunk cut from its source loses the surrounding facts that made it findable — so it surfaces for queries it has nothing to do with.

beats.narration_text: A medical paper split into 20 chunks. Chunk 7: "This treatment reduced mortality by 12%" — no disease named. BM25 hands it to a "diabetes mortality" query on the word match. Prepend "Context: hypertension study in elderly patients" before embedding and it stops matching diabetes queries.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Context-augmented chunks should retrieve precisely for the right query; bare chunks did not; why?
A chunk cut from its source loses the surrounding facts that made it findable — so it surfaces for queries it has nothing to do with.
Generate a short document-level summary and prepend it to each chunk before embedding, so the vector carries both local content and its document context and resolves ambiguous token matches.

beats.narration_text: "mortality reduced 12%" bare → matches cancer/diabetes/heart; with context → matches only hypertension. Precision 33% → 90%.
